channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Pakistan Uses Cement On Vedic-Era Taxila Walls, Gets 'Delisting' Warning From UN

media:content.media:description: The ruins at Taxila reveal the pattern of urban evolution on the Indian subcontinent

item.description: Taxila, located in the Rawalpindi district of Pakistan's Punjab province, is a vast serial site that includes a Mesolithic cave and the archaeological remains of four early settlement sites, Buddhist monasteries, and a mosque and madrassa.
item.content:encoded: Taxila, located in the Rawalpindi district of Pakistan's Punjab province, is a vast serial site that includes a Mesolithic cave and the archaeological remains of four early settlement sites, Buddhist monasteries, and a mosque and madrassa.

item.title: Florida Woman's Driveway Stolen As Part Of Nigerian Scam

media:content.media:description: The missing driveway has been tied to a Nigeria-based scam (Representative pic).

item.description: A Florida woman had her driveway ripped up and taken away as part of an alleged scam.
item.content:encoded: A Florida woman had her driveway ripped up and taken away as part of an alleged scam.

item.title: Israel Wanted To Kill Top Iranian Negotiators. How US Saved Them

media:content.media:description: In March, it was reported that Araghchi and Ghalibaf were on an Israeli target list

item.description: Araghchi and Ghalibaf have been the key officials negotiating with various countries in the region to reach a framework agreement with the US that sought to open the Strait of Hormuz and set the outline for follow-on talks on Tehran's nuclear program.
item.content:encoded: Araghchi and Ghalibaf have been the key officials negotiating with various countries in the region to reach a framework agreement with the US that sought to open the Strait of Hormuz and set the outline for follow-on talks on Tehran's nuclear program.

item.title: "Ridiculous": Trump Criticises "One-Sided Relationship" With NATO

media:content.media:description: Trump has repeatedly lashed out at European allies over their response to the war in Iran

item.description: Donald Trump hit out at European allies over defense spending and the war in Iran as Washington moves to scale back its long-term NATO commitments.
item.content:encoded: Donald Trump hit out at European allies over defense spending and the war in Iran as Washington moves to scale back its long-term NATO commitments.

item.title: Top Iran General Emerges From Hiding Ahead Of Ali Khamenei's Funeral

media:content.media:description: Vahidi himself hasn't been seen publicly since February 8, weeks before the Iran war began (File)

item.description: Gen. Ahmad Vahidi resurfaces after weeks in hiding, joining Iran's leadership as Tehran begins a dayslong funeral for Supreme Leader Khamenei.
item.content:encoded: Gen. Ahmad Vahidi resurfaces after weeks in hiding, joining Iran's leadership as Tehran begins a dayslong funeral for Supreme Leader Khamenei.

item.title: What Makes A Star A Star?

media:content.media:description: Astronomy often learns the most from its rarest objects.

item.description: Stars start out as huge blobs of gas in space - but how big and heavy does a blob of gas have to be before it becomes a star? It sounds like a simple question, but astronomers have debated the answer for decades.
item.content:encoded: Stars start out as huge blobs of gas in space - but how big and heavy does a blob of gas have to be before it becomes a star? It sounds like a simple question, but astronomers have debated the answer for decades.

item.title: "At What Cost?" Congress' Manish Tewari On Letter To PM Modi, Shehbaz Sharif
item.description: Congress MP Manish Tewari questioned calls to resume India-Pakistan dialogue, arguing decades of peace efforts had been met with terror attacks and that talks could not resume till Pak gave verifiable proof of dismantled terror infrastructure.
item.content:encoded: Congress MP Manish Tewari questioned calls to resume India-Pakistan dialogue, arguing decades of peace efforts had been met with terror attacks and that talks could not resume till Pak gave verifiable proof of dismantled terror infrastructure.

item.title: Iran Warns Tankers To Follow Approved Hormuz Routes Or Face Forceful Response

media:content.media:description: Iran-US tensions grow over Hormuz Strait shipping routes amid fragile talks

item.description: Iran threatens forceful response against tankers defying its Hormuz routes, as tensions rise amid US talks and Khamenei's funeral.
item.content:encoded: Iran threatens forceful response against tankers defying its Hormuz routes, as tensions rise amid US talks and Khamenei's funeral.

item.title: Secret Service Missed 102 Radio Calls During 2024 Trump Shooting: Report

media:content.media:description: Trump Rally Shooting: US Secret Service Missed 102 Local Radio Alerts

item.description: A new report exposes severe security failures at the July 2024 Trump rally, revealing the Secret Service missed 102 critical radio alerts.
item.content:encoded: A new report exposes severe security failures at the July 2024 Trump rally, revealing the Secret Service missed 102 critical radio alerts.

item.title: Nearly 3,000 Evacuated As Wildfires Sweep Across France

media:content.media:description: Record heatwave sparks wildfires, forcing 3,000 evacuations across southern France

item.description: Nearly 3,000 people evacuated in southern France as wildfires spread near Sainte-Marie-la-Mer and Canet-en-Roussillon after a record heatwave.
item.content:encoded: Nearly 3,000 people evacuated in southern France as wildfires spread near Sainte-Marie-la-Mer and Canet-en-Roussillon after a record heatwave.

item.title: Ex-Olympian Charged For Causing $1,000 Damage To Trump-Backed Reflecting Pool

media:content.media:description: David Hearn was indicted on a single count of property destruction in Washington, D.C

item.description: Former Olympic canoeist David Hearn faces a felony charge for allegedly ripping up sealant at the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington.
item.content:encoded: Former Olympic canoeist David Hearn faces a felony charge for allegedly ripping up sealant at the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington.

item.title: Man With Tibetan Flag Dies After Setting Himself On Fire Outside UN

media:content.media:description: The man's name has not been released because his family hasn't been notified yet.

item.description: A man holding a Tibetan flag died after self-immolation outside the UN. Over 150 Tibetans have protested Beijing's rule similarly since 2009.
item.content:encoded: A man holding a Tibetan flag died after self-immolation outside the UN. Over 150 Tibetans have protested Beijing's rule similarly since 2009.

item.title: How A 100-Hour Mission By 8 Countries Saved Venezuelan Man After Earthquake

media:content.media:description: Rescuers spent more than 100 hours navigating unstable structure, rain and aftershocks

item.description: The back-to-back quakes, measuring magnitudes 7.2 and 7.5, brought down a seven-storey building and damaged tens of thousands of structures across northern Venezuela.
item.content:encoded: The back-to-back quakes, measuring magnitudes 7.2 and 7.5, brought down a seven-storey building and damaged tens of thousands of structures across northern Venezuela.

item.title: Indian Sailor's Organs Missing From Repatriated Body, New Delhi Seeks Answers From Venezuela

media:content.media:description: In their autopsy report, doctors in Deoria noted that almost all organs of the sailor were missing

item.description: Chauhan's family has alleged that almost all internal organs of the sailor, including the brain, heart, and lungs, were missing.
item.content:encoded: Chauhan's family has alleged that almost all internal organs of the sailor, including the brain, heart, and lungs, were missing.

item.title: Iran To Hold 6-Day Funeral For Ali Khamenei, Son Mojtaba Won't Attend

media:content.media:description: Ali Khamenei's funeral ceremonies will begin in Tehran on July 4 and conclude on July 9.

item.description: Ali Khamenei, who ruled Iran for 36 years, was killed on February 28 - the day the US and Israel began striking Iran, triggering a war in the Middle East.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1121879/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>Ali Khamenei, who ruled Iran for 36 years, was killed on February 28 - the day the US and Israel began striking Iran, triggering a war in the Middle East.

item.title: 'Don't Tell My Wife I'm Alive Yet...': Venezuelan Man's Plea Before Rescue From Rubble

media:content.media:description: The rescue was considered a small miracle cutting through a week of tragedy

item.description: Hernan Alberto Gil Flores emerged to safety covered in dust atop a stretcher surrounded by helmet-clad rescue workers after being trapped since June 24 under rubble
item.content:encoded: Hernan Alberto Gil Flores emerged to safety covered in dust atop a stretcher surrounded by helmet-clad rescue workers after being trapped since June 24 under rubble

item.title: 2 Policemen Killed As Gunmen Open Fire In Pakistan

media:content.media:description: The militants ambushed the police patrol party late on Thursday in Mohmand district. (Representational)

item.description: Two police personnel, including a senior officer, were killed and another critically injured after unidentified gunmen opened fire on a routine police patrol in northwest Pakistan, authorities said on Friday.
item.content:encoded: Two police personnel, including a senior officer, were killed and another critically injured after unidentified gunmen opened fire on a routine police patrol in northwest Pakistan, authorities said on Friday.

item.title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce Donate $26 Million To Charities Ahead Of Wedding

media:content.media:description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce tying the knot on 3 July at Madison Square Garden.

item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have donated $26 million to charities this week ahead of their Friday wedding at Madison Square Garden.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1121720/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have donated $26 million to charities this week ahead of their Friday wedding at Madison Square Garden.

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg Says AI Agent Development Going Slower Than Expected
item.description: Meta Chief Executive Mark Zuckerberg told an internal town hall on Thursday that AI agent development over the last four months has not "accelerated in the way we expected," according to a recording heard by Reuters.
item.content:encoded: Meta Chief Executive Mark Zuckerberg told an internal town hall on Thursday that AI agent development over the last four months has not "accelerated in the way we expected," according to a recording heard by Reuters.

item.title: Turkish Comedian Arrested For Allegedly Insulting Islam

media:content.media:description: Deniz Goktas was arrested at Istanbul Airport on July 2.

item.description: A popular Turkish comedian was arrested on arrival at Istanbul airport on Thursday over a stand-up show that involved material that prosecutors said was allegedly offensive to Islam.
item.content:encoded: A popular Turkish comedian was arrested on arrival at Istanbul airport on Thursday over a stand-up show that involved material that prosecutors said was allegedly offensive to Islam.
